basic_details.job_title: HPCL Graduate Apprentice Trainee Recruitment 2026: Apply Online for 358 Engineering Posts
basic_details.short_information: Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited (HPCL), a Government of India Enterprise under the Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas, has invited online applications for the engagement of Graduate Apprentice Trainees (Engineering) under the Apprentices Act, 1961. The engagement covers Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, and Instrumentation streams across training locations spread all over India for the year 2026-27. Online applications can be submitted from 15 July 2026 to 31 July 2026.

application_fee.details: Application fee details are not specified in the official notification.

qualification.details: Engineering Graduation in Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, or Instrumentation streams, recognized by the Board of Technical Education/UGC/AICTE or equivalent authority. Candidates must have completed their engineering graduation on or after 01-09-2023. Those who completed earlier are not eligible. UR/OBC-NC candidates require 60% aggregate marks; SC/ST/PwBD candidates require 50% aggregate marks, calculated across all semesters/years. Where CGPA/GPA or letter grades are awarded, the percentage must be converted as per the university/institute's norms, supported by a certificate from the university/institute. Only Indian nationals are eligible to apply. Only candidates registered and approved on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS 2.0) portal and by the Board of Apprenticeship Training (BOAT) will be considered. Candidates with an existing apprenticeship contract with any other organization on the NATS portal are not eligible to apply. Candidates with one year or more of work experience are not eligible to apply.

salary_details.details: Selected Graduate Apprentice Trainees (Engineering) will be paid a monthly stipend of Rs. 25,000/-, comprising Rs. 20,500 paid directly by HPCL and Rs. 4,500 paid to apprentices by the Government of India under the DBT scheme. HPCL has no obligation to provide housing assistance or any other allowance beyond this fixed stipend.

selection_process: Applications are screened based on merit percentage as per eligibility criteria and the number of training slots notified. Incomplete or ineligible applications are summarily rejected without intimation. Shortlisted candidates are called for document/certificate verification in a ratio of 1:5 against notified training slots

communicated via registered email. No TA/DA is paid for the verification process. A merit list is prepared based on candidates who report for verification and are found suitable. Selected candidates must accept a tri-partite apprenticeship contract on the NATS website and submit a medical certificate and undergo police verification.
how_to_apply: Register on the Apprentice Application portal at jobs.hpcl.co.in/Recruit_New/recruitlogin.jsp using the "Sign up for New Registration" option.
Login credentials will be sent to the registered email ID by the HPCL Apprentice Team.
Sign in using the received email ID and password.
Select the Engineering Branch from the dropdown as per the branches mentioned in the advertisement.
Fill in Personal Details and click "Validate your details."
Click "Save & Next" to proceed to Contact Details, including correspondence and permanent address.
Select State of Training (only one preference can be selected).
Validate details and proceed to Qualification Details.
Upload a recent passport-sized photograph and signature.
Recheck all details under "Preview" and edit if required.
Read the declaration carefully, check the declaration boxes, and click "Final Submit."
A confirmation email with the HPCL Application Number will be sent after submission.
Note: Candidates are allowed to fill only one application; multiple applications will be rejected.
important_instructions: HPCL does not guarantee permanent or temporary employment after successful completion of apprenticeship. Apprentices who complete the training successfully will receive relaxation/preference in future recruitment norms in two stages: age relaxation to the extent of the apprenticeship period (not exceeding one year), and an additional 5% grace marks in the Computer Based Test (CBT) for shortlisting in subsequent recruitment. Furnishing wrong or false information will lead to disqualification, even after engagement. Canvassing in any form will lead to cancellation of candidature. Apprentices will be placed for training at their preferred state, but may be assigned any training location within that state as per requirement/availability. No hard copy of the application form needs to be submitted to HPCL. All communication will be sent via the registered email ID/mobile number; applications with pseudo/fake email IDs will attract action under the law. HPCL reserves the right to raise minimum eligibility standards, modify the selection process, and change the number of vacancies without prior notice.

rows.details: 358 (computed from state-wise table; not explicitly stated in notification)

content_sections.heading: HPCL Apprentice State-Wise Training Slots
content_sections.text: HPCL has notified the following tentative training slots for Graduate Apprentices, state-wise. Reservation policy will be applied as per the percentage applicable to each respective state. Note: The notification does not provide a branch-wise (Civil/Mechanical/Electrical/Instrumentation) split of slots within each state; this breakup is not specified in the source document.

rows.state: Dadra & Nagar Haveli and Daman & Diu (UT)

content_sections.heading: HPCL Apprentice Educational Qualification and Eligibility
content_sections.text: Engineering Graduation in Civil, Mechanical, Electrical, or Instrumentation streams, recognized by the Board of Technical Education/UGC/AICTE or equivalent authority. Candidates must have completed their engineering graduation on or after 01-09-2023. Those who completed earlier are not eligible. UR/OBC-NC candidates require 60% aggregate marks; SC/ST/PwBD candidates require 50% aggregate marks, calculated across all semesters/years. Where CGPA/GPA or letter grades are awarded, the percentage must be converted as per the university/institute's norms, supported by a certificate from the university/institute. Only Indian nationals are eligible to apply. Only candidates registered and approved on the National Apprenticeship Training Scheme (NATS 2.0) portal and by the Board of Apprenticeship Training (BOAT) will be considered. Candidates with an existing apprenticeship contract with any other organization on the NATS portal are not eligible to apply. Candidates with one year or more of work experience are not eligible to apply.

content_sections.heading: HPCL Apprentice Monthly Stipend
content_sections.text: Selected Graduate Apprentice Trainees (Engineering) will be paid a monthly stipend of Rs. 25,000/- , comprising Rs. 20,500 paid directly by HPCL and Rs. 4,500 paid to apprentices by the Government of India under the DBT scheme. HPCL has no obligation to provide housing assistance or any other allowance beyond this fixed stipend.

content_sections.heading: HPCL Apprentice Selection Process
content_sections.text: Applications are screened based on merit percentage as per eligibility criteria and the number of training slots notified. Incomplete or ineligible applications are summarily rejected without intimation. Shortlisted candidates are called for document/certificate verification in a ratio of 1:5 against notified training slots, communicated via registered email. No TA/DA is paid for the verification process. A merit list is prepared based on candidates who report for verification and are found suitable. Selected candidates must accept a tri-partite apprenticeship contract on the NATS website and submit a medical certificate and undergo police verification.

content_sections.heading: HPCL Apprentice Application Fee
content_sections.text: Application fee details are not specified in the official notification.

content_sections.heading: Documents Required for HPCL Apprentice Application
content_sections.text: Recent passport-sized photograph Signature (for upload) Engineering degree/marksheets for percentage calculation SC/ST/OBC-NC/PwBD certificate in the prescribed format, where applicable (available at www.hpclcareers.com > Downloads > Downloads for Apprentices) Self-certified translated copy of category certificates if issued in a language other than English/Hindi

content_sections.heading: How to Apply Online for HPCL Apprentice Recruitment 2026
content_sections.text: Note: Candidates are allowed to fill only one application; multiple applications will be rejected.

content_sections.heading: HPCL Apprentice Important Instructions
content_sections.text: HPCL does not guarantee permanent or temporary employment after successful completion of apprenticeship. Apprentices who complete the training successfully will receive relaxation/preference in future recruitment norms in two stages: age relaxation to the extent of the apprenticeship period (not exceeding one year), and an additional 5% grace marks in the Computer Based Test (CBT) for shortlisting in subsequent recruitment. Furnishing wrong or false information will lead to disqualification, even after engagement. Canvassing in any form will lead to cancellation of candidature. Apprentices will be placed for training at their preferred state, but may be assigned any training location within that state as per requirement/availability. No hard copy of the application form needs to be submitted to HPCL. All communication will be sent via the registered email ID/mobile number; applications with pseudo/fake email IDs will attract action under the law. HPCL reserves the right to raise minimum eligibility standards, modify the selection process, and change the number of vacancies without prior notice.

faq.question: Q1. What is the last date to apply for HPCL apprentice recruitment 2026?
faq.answer: The online application closes on 31 July 2026.

faq.question: Q2. What is the monthly stipend for HPCL Graduate Apprentice Trainee?
faq.answer: Rs. 25,000/- per month (Rs. 20,500 from HPCL and Rs. 4,500 from GOI under the DBT scheme).

faq.question: Q3. What is the age limit for HPCL apprentice recruitment?
faq.answer: 18 to 25 years as on 31-07-2026, with relaxation for SC/ST, OBC-NC, and PwBD candidates.

faq.question: Q4. Is HPCL apprenticeship a permanent job?
faq.answer: No. HPCL does not commit to permanent or temporary employment after apprenticeship completion, though relaxation/preference is given in future recruitment.

faq.question: Q5. Which engineering branches are eligible for HPCL apprentice recruitment?
Q6. Can candidates with work experience apply?
faq.answer: No. Candidates with one year or more of work experience are not eligible.
